title: AutoStream Submission Mode — Full Compliance Build
objective: Bring this repository to full submission-ready status with strict compliance to the assignment rubric, minimal code changes, correct architecture, and verified end-to-end behavior.

mandatory_stack.framework: LangGraph preferred; LangChain acceptable; AutoGen acceptable
mandatory_stack.llm: GPT-4o-mini or Gemini 1.5 Flash or Claude 3 Haiku
mandatory_stack.state_management: Must retain memory across 5-6 conversation turns using LangGraph state, memory buffers, or an equivalent persistent session store
mandatory_stack.memory_implementation: A persistent memory layer must exist in code and survive multi-turn conversations
mandatory_stack.rag: Local knowledge base only for pricing, features, and policies
mandatory_stack.tool_execution: mock_lead_capture must run only after name, email, and platform are all collected

behavior_rules: Do not rewrite the entire project.
Do not waste time on unnecessary explanations.
Do not remove working deterministic logic.
Do not introduce extra dependencies unless required for the rubric.
Do not let the LLM answer factual KB questions.
Do not trigger lead capture early.
Do not stop at partial fixes.
hard_constraints: No hallucinated pricing or policy answers.
execution_order: 1. Audit the full repository structure and identify all gaps against the assignment.
4. Add or confirm one allowed LLM integration.
10. Update README to match the actual implementation.
11. Validate the full demo flow end-to-end.

python_compatibility: Replace all 3.10+ typing syntax with Python 3.9-safe equivalents.
Use Optional, Union, Dict, List where needed.
framework_compliance: Ensure LangGraph or LangChain or AutoGen is present in the actual codebase.
Prefer LangGraph for orchestration if the architecture is stateful.
llm_compliance: Integrate one allowed LLM only if needed for compliance.
Keep factual KB answers deterministic and local.
Use the LLM only for soft natural-language generation if at all.
state_management: Retain lead fields until tool execution.
Support at least 5-6 turns of context.
rag_compliance: Answer pricing, features, and policies only from the local knowledge base.
Do not use the LLM for factual retrieval.
tooling_compliance: Ask for name, email, and platform only when high intent is detected.
Call mock_lead_capture exactly once and only after all required fields are present.

commands: Convert all Python 3.10+ syntax to Python 3.9-compatible syntax.
Fix typing issues and import issues.
Add minimal LangChain or LLM integration if required.
Verify KB answers are exact and local.
Verify lead capture gating is strict.
Include only runtime dependencies needed to run the project.
Exclude dev-only tools unless truly required.
Update README to match actual implementation.
Greeting -> pricing question -> KB answer -> high intent -> name -> email -> platform -> lead capture.

acceptance_criteria: Lead capture tool is correctly gated.
README and requirements.txt match the codebase.

execution_style.goal: Pass evaluation with zero missing rubric items
